text.title: feedback-management Core Rules
text.summary: Migrated testable rules for feedback-management. Verify and refine before relying on for production.

rule.statement: All feedback ingest sources (support tickets, NPS, sales calls, app-store reviews) MUST be mapped to standardized fields: source, date, user_id, segment, verbatim. Never ingest raw data without annotation.
rule.rationale: Annotated ingest lets agents weight by segment and avoid false-positive clustering from identical generic complaints across different sources.

rule.statement: The taxonomy is FIXED and closed. TYPE: [bug|request|enhancement|confusion|praise|complaint]. TOPIC: [onboarding|core-A|core-B|billing|integrations|performance|other]. SEGMENT: [free|solo|pro|geek|unknown]. SENTIMENT: [positive|neutral|negative]. Agents must not invent new tags; violations surface as validation errors.
rule.rationale: Fixed taxonomy prevents drift. LLMs invent new tags ("UI/UX", "minor bug") even when given an enum — enforce with JSON schema validation, not prompt politeness.

rule.statement: Every feedback item MUST include the original verbatim alongside tags. Never let the agent rewrite the user's words — re-tagging later is impossible without the source string.
rule.rationale: Verbatim preservation is non-negotiable. Close-loop emails must quote the user's original phrasing; paraphrasing erodes trust.

rule.statement: Weight aggregations by ARR / retention risk / segment, not raw mention count. A loud free-tier user is not the same signal as a silent enterprise account.
rule.rationale: Raw mention count rewards the squeaky wheel. Segment weighting reveals true product priorities beneath noise.

rule.statement: Run the triage agent at single-item granularity, not in batches. Batch prompts hide per-item confidence scores and cause the model to over-cluster.
rule.rationale: Single-item triage lets the agent report confidence honestly. Batch processing produces false-confidence outputs when the model averages across items.

rule.statement: Require a confidence field; route low-confidence items (confidence less than 0.7) to a human queue, not to auto-action.
rule.rationale: Confidence routing prevents high-risk mis-categorizations from driving roadmap decisions.

rule.statement: Keep close-loop emails as DRAFTS in the human's inbox, never auto-send. Trust collapses fast if a generated reply misquotes the user.
rule.rationale: Human review gates all outbound communication. Drafts let the PM fix tone or correct agent errors before customer contact.

rule.statement: Link every backlog item back to its source feedback IDs (foreign key, not free-text). Lets you re-contact requesters when shipped.
rule.rationale: Traceability lets the team close the loop with specific requesters. Without it, feedback and backlog are disconnected.

rule.statement: Re-run categorization quarterly on the historical corpus when the taxonomy evolves — agents make this cheap, humans don't.
rule.rationale: Quarterly re-tagging keeps historical data consistent with evolved taxonomy. Cheap agent processing enables this audit.

rule.statement: Keep a "rejected suggestions" log with the honest reason. Builds trust and prevents the same idea recurring through the funnel.
rule.rationale: Transparency in rejection prevents users from feeling unheard. Public archive of why ideas were rejected builds long-term trust.

rule.statement: Pin the taxonomy in the system prompt and in a JSON schema; reject any agent output with tags outside the enum. Drift kills aggregation.
rule.rationale: Enum validation is non-negotiable. Agents will drift taxonomy unless the schema blocks unknown tags.

rule.statement: Embedding-based dedup gives false positives on short generic complaints ("slow", "broken"); always require a length floor (~30 chars) or include thread context.
rule.rationale: Short complaints are generic; dedup requires context. Do not collapse "slow" + "broken" into one data point without evidence they describe the same bug.

rule.statement: Sentiment models read sarcasm wrong ~20% of the time; never use sentiment alone to gate priority.
rule.rationale: Sentiment is a signal, not a gate. Combine with mention count and segment weighting before making roadmap moves.

rule.statement: Agents conflate prospects, active users, and churned users. Lifecycle stage must be a required input field, not optional.
rule.rationale: Prospect feedback is signal about messaging, not product. Churned-user feedback is too late for iteration. Separate these signals early.

rule.statement: "Top requests by mention count" output flatters the agent and the loud minority simultaneously. Force a segment-weighted secondary ranking. Top requests = [sum(mentions × weight_segment × weight_arr)].
rule.rationale: Weighting prevents loud free-tier users from dominating. Segment weighting + ARR weighting surfaces true product priorities.

rule.statement: Close-loop drafts hallucinate dates ("you mentioned this last quarter") if the source date isn't passed in. Pass it; verify it.
rule.rationale: Date verification prevents drafts from claiming false recency. Pass source date into the close-loop drafting prompt.

rule.statement: Auto-creating Linear/Productboard items on every feedback-tagged ticket floods the backlog. Require a clustering step (≥3 mentions same theme) before issue creation.
rule.rationale: Clustering gates backlog creation. Prevents every piece of feedback from becoming a ticket.

rule.statement: Long agent context windows tempt "load all 6 months of feedback at once" — accuracy drops sharply past ~100 items per call. Chunk and reduce.
rule.rationale: Agent accuracy degrades in long contexts. Chunk feedback by month or theme before synthesis.

rule.statement: Close-the-loop emails MUST quote the user's original verbatim verbatim (not paraphrased), reference the date, and link to the changelog. Do not send — return drafts for human review.
rule.rationale: Verbatim quotes prove the team was listening. Paraphrase erodes trust. Human review gates tone and correctness.

rule.statement: Central feedback store MUST support REST API and webhooks for ingest. Recommended: Productboard (strongest object model), Canny (public boards + voting), Linear (as backlog destination, not primary store). Notion OK for less than 500 items/month.
rule.rationale: REST + webhooks let agents push/pull feedback programmatically. Avoid tools that require CSV exports as the primary integration path.

rule.statement: Feedback ingest sources (Intercom, Zendesk, app-store scraper, Slack) all pipe to the triage agent first. Never let raw data bypass categorization.
rule.rationale: Uniform triage entry ensures consistent taxonomy regardless of source.

rule.statement: Use Linear/Jira as the backlog destination, NOT as the feedback store. Link insights from Productboard/Canny into Linear via custom field. This separation keeps insights queryable and issues focused on execution.
rule.rationale: Feedback stores are built for clustering and synthesis. Issue trackers are built for execution. Mixing them degrades both.

rule.statement: Dovetail is strongest for qualitative interview transcripts and coding, not real-time feedback. Use it in tandem with a feedback store: Dovetail for discovery data, Productboard for inbound.
rule.rationale: Tool specialization. Each tool is best for its domain.

rule.statement: Custom feedback-triage-agent (Haiku): Categorize one item at a time against a fixed taxonomy. Cheap, fast, parallelizable. Enforce JSON schema validation to block taxonomy drift.
rule.rationale: Haiku is cost-effective for high-volume triage. Single-item processing lets the agent report confidence honestly.

rule.statement: Custom feedback-aggregator-agent (Sonnet): Weekly/monthly: cluster, count, weight by segment, output ranked themes with verbatim citations. Never auto-cluster without human review.
rule.rationale: Sonnet is strong at structured synthesis. Weekly aggregations build PM-visible signal from daily noise.

rule.statement: faion-mlp-gap-finder-agent: Already referenced in the README; consume aggregated feedback to surface gaps between current MLP and user requests.
rule.rationale: Feedback → MLP gap is a key PM decision cycle. Automate this synthesis.

rule.statement: faion-sdd-executor-agent: Once a feedback theme becomes a backlog item, hand to this agent for spec/design generation in `.aidocs/backlog/`.
rule.rationale: Feedback → spec is the downstream motion. Automation here shortens the loop from discovery to roadmap.

rule.statement: brainstorm skill: For open-ended "what does this batch of confused-onboarding feedback really mean?" diverge-converge when triage is ambiguous.
rule.rationale: Brainstorming before categorization sometimes yields better taxonomy or reveals new patterns.

rule.statement: linear-cli (`@linear/cli`): Create/update issues from feedback themes via Linear API. Never auto-create; require human approval.
rule.rationale: Backlog automation gates creation. Linear becomes the source of truth for roadmap, not a dump for every feedback mention.

rule.statement: gh (GitHub CLI): Mine GitHub Issues + Discussions for feedback signal with `gh issue list --label feedback`.
rule.rationale: GitHub is a feedback source if your product is open-source. CLI lets agents batch-process feedback from GH.

rule.statement: jq: Parse triage agent JSON output before piping to issue tracker. Validate schema early.
rule.rationale: jq validation gates bad data from reaching the tracker. Early validation prevents cascading errors.

rule.statement: intercom-cli (community) / zendesk zcli: Pull conversations tagged `feedback` for batch ingest. Never leave feedback stuck in support tools.
rule.rationale: Centralization is critical. Feedback locked in support tools becomes orphaned. Ingest into the feedback store.

rule.statement: play-scraper / app-store-scraper: Pull app-store reviews in JSON. Pipe directly into triage agent.
rule.rationale: App store reviews are high-signal feedback. Automate their ingestion instead of manual reading.

rule.statement: slack-cli: Pull `#feedback` channel messages, react with status emoji. Keep team aligned on what's being triaged.
rule.rationale: Slack channel → feedback store pipeline lets the team see feedback movement in real-time.
